response.justification: Rodriguez has a legitimate CS degree, lists Java as a skill, and demonstrates solid agile practice (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development), but is fundamentally mismatched with this role on three hard criteria: German is listed as 'Basic' while the role requires strong verbal and written German for client-facing work; 15+ years of Staff and Senior-level experience makes her severely overqualified for an entry-level position; and Java is a minor, dated entry in her skill set — her career has been built primarily on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. No insurance sector exposure further reduces contextual fit.
strengths: CS degree and over a decade of professional software development with demonstrated OOP and agile practices
Cloud exposure via AWS CDK and container/Kubernetes infrastructure directly relevant to the required cloud technologies
Java listed as a skill with documented usage at Altom Consulting, including work on robotic arms and mobile device tooling
concerns: German proficiency is explicitly 'Basic' — the role requires strong verbal and written German for client collaboration
Staff/Senior-level experience spanning 15+ years makes this an extreme overqualification for an entry-level Junior role
Core technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js; Java is peripheral and last used circa 2016–2018
